topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box, tucked away in the attic, smelled faintly of old paper and lavender. Liam had been tasked with clearing out some forgotten relics for his grandmother, and this box, labeled simply "School Stuff," had caught his eye. Inside, nestled amongst yellowed notebooks and dried-out crayons, was a meticulously organized collection of every assignment he had ever turned in since kindergarten. His breath hitched. He pulled out a crumpled essay from the third grade—a masterpiece of poorly formed sentences—and a pristine, color-coded project from high school. A flush crept up his neck, warm and undeniable. He quickly shoved the box back into the shadows, his fingers trembling slightly as he tried to regain his composure. The sheer volume of his academic history, laid bare like a shameful confession, made him want to disappear.

[story 2]

"Seriously, Mom?" I muttered, staring at the stack of graded tests spilling out of her desk drawer. It was an avalanche of homework, every single paper, every worksheet, neatly filed. My cheeks burned. I felt a sudden, intense urge to physically retreat, to shrink into the floorboards. How could she keep all of it? It felt like an audit of my entire existence. I picked up a particularly childish drawing and immediately dropped it, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet room. I ran a hand through my hair, trying to smooth down the sudden dampness on my forehead, desperately wishing the floor would swallow me whole.

[story 3]

It was a Tuesday afternoon, and while searching for a misplaced library book, Maya stumbled upon a large, taped-up storage bin labeled "Academic Archives." Inside, the contents were startling: a chronological record of her scholastic output, from primary school reports to college applications. A wave of profound awkwardness washed over her. She picked up a detailed physics lab report from sophomore year, her fingers brushing against the crisp paper, and felt a distinct tightening in her chest. She quickly dropped the document, her gaze darting around the room as if expecting someone to materialize and witness this monumental oversight. A shaky exhale escaped her lips; the weight of all those completed tasks felt suddenly unbearable.
